L1.area: Cryptographic signing state management and persistence

L2.description: A race condition exists in the consensus signature state handling. When two signing requests arrive nearly simultaneously for the same block height, round, and step, a split read-write lock pattern allows both requests to pass safety checks before either updates the state. This enables conflicting signatures to be produced for the same block, constituting a double-sign protocol violation that results in stake slashing penalties.

L3.area: Cryptographic signing state management and persistence
L3.description: A race condition exists in the consensus signature state handling. When two signing requests arrive nearly simultaneously for the same block height, round, and step, a split read-write lock pattern allows both requests to pass safety checks before either updates the state. This enables conflicting signatures to be produced for the same block, constituting a double-sign protocol violation that results in stake slashing penalties.

ground_truth.reason: The Save method performed a safety check and state update under separate locks, allowing two concurrent signing requests for the same height-round-step to both pass the check before either wrote the updated state, resulting in a double-sign.
